title: euphorbia humilis
intro: Euphorbia humilis, also known as the creeping spurge or dwarf spurge, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the Euphorbiaceae family. It is native to tropical Africa and is characterized by its small, rounded leaves and stems that can grow up to 1 meter in length. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's medicinal properties can be extracted through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, from minor cuts and scrapes to more serious conditions like arthritis.
